pr.title: Create `decomp.py` as an abstraction over m2c
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Added `tools/decomp.py`, a command-line wrapper around m2c that locates the object file containing a requested function, optionally regenerates `build/ctx.c` with m2ctx, runs m2c with the correct target/context/function/ASM path, and can copy, print, or colorize the output. The PR also added symbol aliases needed by the tooling, expanded Python virtualenv ignores, and corrected an m2ctx help string from colorize to format.
what_changed: Created executable `tools/decomp.py` with argparse usage `decomp.py [--no-context] [--no-copy] [--no-print] [--colorize] function ...`.
Implemented object discovery by scanning `build/GALE01/obj/**/*.o` and using pyelftools to check each `.symtab` for an `STT_FUNC` symbol whose name matches the requested function.
Derived the assembly input path from the matching object path by taking the object-relative path and changing its suffix to `.s` under `build/GALE01/asm`.

Wrapped m2c invocation with fixed project defaults: `python tools/m2c/m2c.py`, `--target ppc-mwcc-c`, `--context build/ctx.c`, `--function <name>`, and the resolved assembly file, while forwarding remaining positional arguments to m2c.
Added optional context generation through `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py --quiet --preprocessor`, controlled by `--no-context`.
Added optional clipboard copy via `pyperclip`, optional stdout printing, and optional Pygments terminal colorization for generated C output.

Added several symbol entries/aliases in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, including `fn_800051CC`, `__OSDBINTSTART`, `fn_803435B0`, `__OSDBJUMPEND`, `__OSEVStart`, `__OSSystemCallVectorStart`, `__init_cpp_exceptions_reference`, and `__destroy_global_chain_reference`.
Expanded `.gitignore` Python environment patterns to include `.env`, `.venv`, `env/`, `ENV/`, `env.bak/`, and `venv.bak/`.
Fixed `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py` argparse help text for `--format` from `colorize the output` to `format the output`.
smart_moves: The wrapper removes repeated manual steps from the decomp workflow: finding the object file, selecting the corresponding ASM path, regenerating context, passing the correct m2c target/context/function flags, and copying the result.
Using ELF symbol tables for function lookup is more robust than guessing file paths from names or requiring the user to know the object module containing a function.
Returning object paths relative to `obj_root` and reusing that relative path under `asm_root` preserves the build tree layout and keeps object-to-assembly mapping simple.
The design forwards unknown trailing arguments to m2c via `argparse.REMAINDER`, so the abstraction does not block advanced m2c options.
The script uses resolved absolute paths when invoking subprocesses, reducing sensitivity to the caller's current working directory.
The tool fails fast with the exact command and stderr when subprocesses fail, which is useful for diagnosing m2ctx/m2c/toolchain problems.
Optional `--no-context`, `--no-copy`, `--no-print`, and `--colorize` flags let users tune the workflow for quick iteration, scripted use, or terminal review.
decomp_lessons: For decompilation helper scripts, prefer deriving paths from the repository root and build layout rather than requiring developers to pass object and ASM paths manually.
When a function-to-file mapping is needed, pyelftools plus `.symtab` `STT_FUNC` lookup can automate discovery from built objects.
A thin project-specific wrapper around generic decomp tools can encode stable defaults such as `--target ppc-mwcc-c` and the shared generated context path.
If a workflow depends on generated context, make regeneration the default but provide a skip flag for fast repeated runs.
Keep wrapper tools extensible by forwarding additional arguments to the underlying tool rather than re-declaring every supported option.

Symbol aliases and labels in `symbols.txt` can matter for tooling even when they do not directly add matched C code; aliases around OS exception/system-call vectors and ctor/dtor references were added here alongside the wrapper.
Small CLI help text accuracy matters because decomp tools are often driven from terminal workflows and copied usage output.
naming_conventions: New unknown/generated function labels follow the existing `fn_<ADDRESS>` style, e.g. `fn_800051CC` and `fn_803435B0`.
OS SDK-style label aliases preserve double-underscore and capitalization patterns such as `__OSDBINTSTART`, `__OSDBINTEND`, `__OSDBJUMPEND`, `__OSEVStart`, and `__OSSystemCallVectorStart`.
C++ runtime reference symbols use descriptive double-underscore names ending in `_reference`, e.g. `__init_cpp_exceptions_reference` and `__destroy_global_chain_reference`.
The new helper script is named `decomp.py`, reflecting its role as a project-level decompilation command rather than an m2c-specific script name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The wrapper scans built `.o` files for the requested `STT_FUNC` symbol, then maps the matching object file to the corresponding `.s` file under `build/GALE01/asm`.
m2c is invoked with `--target ppc-mwcc-c`, matching the project's PowerPC/MWCC C decompilation target.
The generated context file is standardized as `build/ctx.c` and produced by m2ctx with `--quiet --preprocessor` before running m2c unless `--no-context` is supplied.
The output can be copied directly to the clipboard, supporting the common tactic of quickly pasting m2c output into a source file for iterative matching.
Colorized terminal output via Pygments is optional, useful for visual inspection without changing the generated code.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify or rely on `tools/decomp.py`.
Search for documented usage of `decomp.py --no-context` or forwarded m2c arguments in developer workflows.
Check whether additional dependencies such as `pyelftools`, `pyperclip`, or `pygments` were documented or added to setup instructions in later PRs.
Investigate whether the symbol aliases added in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` fixed specific m2ctx/m2c context generation issues.
